# Example of using fuzzing within mcpscripttest
# This shows how to integrate fuzzing into your test workflow

# Start a test server
mcp-server-start timeserver -- go run ./examples/servers/mcp-time-server

# Run a simple fuzzing test
exec go test -v -run TestSimpleFuzzing -fuzz=Fuzz -fuzztime=5s ./fuzzing/examples
stdout 'fuzzing'
! stderr 'FAIL'

# Test with coverage enabled
env GOCOVERDIR=$WORK/coverage
mkdir coverage

# Run fuzzing with coverage collection
exec go test -v -run TestCoverageFuzzing -fuzz=Fuzz -fuzztime=5s ./fuzzing/examples
stdout 'coverage'
stdout 'fuzzing'

# Analyze coverage results
exec go tool covdata percent -i coverage
stdout '%'

# Use the Run function for standalone fuzzing
exec go run ./fuzzing/examples/standalone_fuzz.go
stdout 'Running standalone fuzzing'
stdout 'Scripts tested:'

# Test with specialized generators
exec go run ./fuzzing/examples/specialized_fuzz.go
stdout 'Using MCP trace generator'
stdout 'mcp-trace'
stdout 'Scripts generated:'

# Example of cooperative fuzzing
exec go build -o test_tool ./examples/test_echo
exec go run ./fuzzing/examples/cooperative_fuzz.go ./test_tool
stdout 'Cooperative fuzzing with test_tool'
stdout 'Generated valid commands'
